source.definition: to develop or present something in detail and with thoroughness

explanation.sourceText: The verb 'elaborate' comes from the Latin root 'elaborare', meaning 'to work out carefully'. It conveys the idea of carefully developing or refining something in detail.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „elaborate“ provine din rădăcina latină „elaborare“, care înseamnă „a lucra cu atenție“. Transmite ideea de a dezvolta sau rafina ceva în detaliu și cu grijă.

source.additional: Regular verb. Commonly used to describe the detailed development of texts, plans, or concepts.

target.definition: a dezvolta sau a formula ceva în detaliu

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian verb „a elabora“ derives from the Latin „elaborare“, meaning „to work carefully“ or „to develop“. It is commonly used in academic and creative contexts.
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a elabora“ derivă din latinescul „elaborare“, care înseamnă „a lucra cu atenție“ sau „a dezvolta“. Este folosit în contexte academice și creative.

target.additional: Regular first conjugation verb (-a). Frequently used in academic and creative contexts.

explanation.sourceText: Means to develop something thoroughly and in detail.
explanation.targetText: Înseamnă a dezvolta ceva în mod detaliat și atent.

examples.sourceText: I am elaborating the report.

explanation.sourceText: Often used in research and planning.
explanation.targetText: Este folosit frecvent în cercetare și planificare.

examples.sourceText: We are working out a new strategy.
I am elaborating the report.
Are you working on the project?
He is elaborating the plan.
We are elaborating the concept.
Are you also working on the presentation?
They are working on the new project.
I have elaborated the strategy.
They have elaborated the solution.
He worked on the project every day.
examples.targetText: El elabora proiectul în fiecare zi.

examples.sourceText: I had already elaborated the idea when you came.
examples.targetText: Elaborasem deja ideea când ai venit.

examples.sourceText: I will elaborate the concept tomorrow.
They will elaborate the presentation later.
examples.targetText: Ei o să elaboreze prezentarea mai târziu.

examples.sourceText: I would elaborate the work if I had time.
examples.targetText: Aș elabora lucrarea dacă aș avea timp.

examples.sourceText: It is important that you elaborate the details.
Elaborate the concept!
Elaborate the plans!
The elaborated presentation was impressive.
The results are elaborated.
By elaborating the concept, I improve my skills.

learningTips.text: The English word 'elaborate' means to develop something carefully and in detail, just like the Romanian 'a elabora'.
Remember 'elaborate' by thinking of 'labor'—working hard to develop an idea fully.
Think of an artist elaborating a painting by adding fine details, similar to how 'a elabora' means to develop something thoroughly.
The prefix 'e-' in 'elaborate' can remind you of 'ex-' meaning 'out' or 'thoroughly', helping you connect it to the Romanian 'a elabora'.
